steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage establishes facts about the Federalist Papers (what they are, who wrote them, when and where published, why they mattered), then pivots to something not completely known: the authorship of certain essays. The correct choice must reflect this structure — established facts followed by a specific authorship uncertainty.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — mischaracterizes the authorship question: does not accurately reflect the uncertainty described in the passage. ✗
  • Choice B — focuses only on publication details: omits the authorship uncertainty that is central to the passage's pivot. ✗
  • Choice C — invents authorial disagreement: the passage does not describe the authors disagreeing with each other. ✗
  • Choice D — notes that No. 62's authorship is uncertain (Hamilton or Madison), while No. 6 is definitively Hamilton's: accurately captures both the established facts and the specific unresolved authorship question. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
